---
title: "LRPGF06b: Gain factors for phoneme blending (blending) (B), treated-only"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8).
:::

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
factor_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "factor_summary.csv")
```

## Overview

LRPGF06b is a treated-only **gain-factors** model for phoneme blending (blending) (`blending`): what is associated with how much children gain across the three period transitions (ANCOVA - the period post-count given its pre-count - Beta-Binomial logit, child random intercept). It conditions on the own baseline, age, cognitive ability (block design), the upstream DAG skill letter sounds (L), with focal interactions group$\times$ability, group$\times$own-baseline, age$\times$ability.

::: {.callout-important title="Causal labelling (DAG guardrails)"}

Once restricted to on-intervention rows the treatment indicator is constant, so it and its interactions are dropped: every coefficient here is an **adjusted association** among the treated (confounded by latent GA), never a treatment effect. Intervention sessions/dose are never conditioned on (a DAG collider). SES is excluded (not a DAG node; statistically redundant).

:::

## Model

Beta-Binomial$(N, \mu\kappa, (1-\mu)\kappa)$ on the period post-count, $N = 10$, with
$\operatorname{logit}\mu = \alpha + \alpha_{\text{period}} + \gamma_{\text{own}}\operatorname{logit}_*(\text{pre}) + \gamma_A A_{\text{std}} + \gamma_{\text{ability}}z(\text{blocks})  + $\sum_s\gamma_s\operatorname{logit}_*(s_{\text{pre}})$ for $s\in\{L\}$ + \sum\text{interactions} + u_{\text{child}}$. Priors: shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=24%}
![tau (treatment)](prior_tau.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_own](prior_gamma_own.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_age](prior_gamma_age.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_cross](prior_gamma_cross.svg){width=24%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.svg){width=24%}

## Prior-predictive check

![Prior predictive](prior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Posterior-predictive check

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Findings

### Factor summary

```{python}
# | echo: false
factor_summary
```

`role` is **association** for every row — this is a treated-only model (restricted to on-intervention rows, so the treatment indicator is constant and no randomised on-intervention term is estimated); `mean`/`lo`/`hi` are the posterior mean and equal-tailed 95% CrI (logit scale); `prob_positive` = $P(\text{coef}>0)$. Interactions are expected to be weakly identified (wide CrI) at $n\approx54$.

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
